615	/-! ### Cast-collapse of `pullbackCongr` against the free-pullback comparisons
616	
617	The three transports of the bundle cocycle are interleaved with `pullbackCongr` casts
618	(the `glueData_bridge_*` endpoint alignments). The next three lemmas collapse those casts
619	against the free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. Each is generic in the (equal)
620	base morphisms and proved by `subst`, so applying them never forces the kernel to whnf a
621	concrete immersion (the `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` discipline). -/
622	
623	/-- Closed zig-zag: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = 𝟙` for equal base morphisms
624	`φ = ψ`. Project-local helper for the C2 endpoint alignment. -/
625	@[reassoc]
626	lemma pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr_hom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
627	    (I : Type u) :
628	    (pullbackFreeIso φ I).inv ≫
629	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).app
630	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)).hom ≫
631	        (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
632	      = 𝟙 _ := by
633	  subst h
634	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
635	
636	/-- Left absorption: `pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = Q_φ` for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`.
637	Project-local helper for the C2 endpoint alignment (source bridge). -/
638	@[reassoc]
639	lemma pullbackCongr_hom_app_free {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
640	    (I : Type u) :
641	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).app
642	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)).hom ≫
643	        (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
644	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
645	  subst h
646	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
647	
648	/-- Right absorption: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app = Q_ψ⁻¹` for equal base morphisms
649	`φ = ψ`. Project-local helper for the C2 endpoint alignment (target bridge). -/
650	@[reassoc]
651	lemma pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
652	    (I : Type u) :
653	    (pullbackFreeIso φ I).inv ≫
654	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).app
655	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)).hom
656	      = (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).inv := by
657	  subst h
658	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
659	
660	/-- Inverse-side absorption of the congruence cast against the free-pullback comparison:
661	`pullbackCongr(h).inv.app ≫ Q_φ = Q_ψ` for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`. Generic-`subst`
662	companion of `pullbackCongr_hom_app_free`. Project-local helper for the tautological
663	quotient overlap. -/
664	@[reassoc]
665	lemma pullbackCongr_inv_app_free {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
666	    (I : Type u) :
667	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).inv.app
668	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
669	        (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom
670	      = (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom := by
671	  subst h
672	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
673	
674	/-- Cancellation of the pseudofunctor-composition cast against the pulled-back source
675	comparison: `(pullbackComp b a).inv.app (free) ≫ (pullback b).map Q_a = Q_{b≫a} ≫ Q_b⁻¹`.
676	Direct consequence of the free coherence `pullbackFreeIso_comp`. Project-local helper for
677	the tautological quotient overlap. -/
678	@[reassoc]
679	lemma pullbackComp_inv_app_free_map {V U X : Scheme.{u}} (b : V ⟶ U) (a : U ⟶ X)
680	    (I : Type u) :
681	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv.app
682	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := X.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
683	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom
684	      = (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom ≫ (pullbackFreeIso b I).inv := by
685	  rw [Iso.eq_comp_inv, Category.assoc]
686	  -- `erw` (defeq matching) to fire the free coherence through the `X.Modules` diamond
687	  erw [← pullbackFreeIso_comp a b I]
688	  exact Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a) _ _
689	
690	/-! ### Adjunction transposition of the descent-equalizer legs
691	
692	The overlap condition consumed by `glueLift` is an equation between composites of adjoint
693	transposes along the chart immersions. The next two lemmas transpose each leg back across
694	the pullback–pushforward adjunction of the *composite* overlap immersion, exposing the
695	pullback-level identity `g_{ij} ∘ f_ij^* u^i = (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* u^j` that the matrix
696	computation closes. The first handles the unit/`pushforwardComp` factor pair, the second
697	the trailing `pushforwardCongr` reindexing cast. -/
698	
699	/-- **Leg transpose** (`lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose` engine): for

44	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
45	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
46	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
47	      congr 1)
48	
49	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
50	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
51	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
52	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
53	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
54	
55	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
56	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
57	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
58	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
59	  ext Y
60	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
61	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
62	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
63	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
64	  exact map_one _
65	
66	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
67	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
68	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
69	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
70	  ext Y
71	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
72	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
73	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
74	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
75	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
76	  rfl
77	
78	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
79	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
80	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
81	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
82	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
83	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
87	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
88	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
89	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
90	
91	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
92	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
93	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
94	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
95	  exact one_smul _ _
96	
97	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
98	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
99	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
100	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
101	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
102	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
103	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
104	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
105	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
106	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
107	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
108	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
109	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
110	
111	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
112	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
113	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
114	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
115	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
116	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
117	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
118	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
119	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
121	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
122	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
123	
124	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
125	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
126	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
127	  classical
128	  induction s using Finset.induction with
129	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
130	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
131	
132	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
133	
134	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
135	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
136	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
137	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
138	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
139	
140	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
141	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
142	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
143	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts

744	  rw [hnat]
745	  -- The RHS pushforward (its `map`-application is `rfl` on sections) evaluates the
746	  -- scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd (p.appTop a)` at `1` over the preimage open; both sides are
747	  -- then `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)` (the `homOfLE`s agree by
748	  -- proof irrelevance, the comorphism by `forget₂`-on-elements), so `scalarEnd_val_app_one` closes.
749	  exact (scalarEnd_val_app_one ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a)
750	    (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map p.base).obj (Opposite.unop Y)))).symm
751	
752	/-- **ATOM: scalar endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`).
753	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `a ∈ Γ(S,⊤)`, pulling back the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` is,
754	after the unit-pullback comparison `q = pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the
755	base-changed function `p.appTop a`:
756	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ q = q ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`.
757	Proved by transposing under the pullback-pushforward adjunction to
758	`unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`.
759	Project-local — the single irreducible new claim underlying `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
760	lemma scalarEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
761	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫
762	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
763	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
764	        scalarEnd (p.appTop a) := by
765	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
766	    (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) |>.injective
767	  -- `homEquiv_naturality_left` collapses the LHS transpose; the RHS transpose
768	  -- (`homEquiv_naturality_right`) is supplied as a term because positional `rw` cannot match
769	  -- the identical-printing `homEquiv` under the `X.Modules` diamond (memory
770	  -- `grquot-functor-dropped-termmode`).
771	  -- `hq` is the lemma `..._homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` restated in the `Scheme.Modules`
772	  -- adjunction form (defeq to the `SheafOfModules` form), so `rw`/`congrArg` match syntactically.
773	  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
774	        (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
775	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
776	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) :=
777	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit _
778	  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, hq]
779	  refine (unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm p a).trans ?_
780	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv_naturality_right
781	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
782	        (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).trans
783	      (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))) hq)).symm
784	
785	/-- The action of `matrixEnd M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEnd M`
786	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
787	for `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
788	lemma ιFree_matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
789	    (j : Fin d) :
790	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEnd M
791	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
792	  rw [matrixEnd, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
793	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
794	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
795	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
796	  rfl
797	
798	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
799	/-- **(a) Matrix endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback`).
800	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S,⊤)`, the pullback of the matrix
801	endomorphism `matrixEnd M` is, after the free-pullback comparison `Q = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`,
802	the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p.appTop • M` (entrywise comorphism):
803	`(pullback p).map (matrixEnd M) = Q.hom ≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop.mapMatrix M) ≫ Q.inv`.
804	Reduces, on each one-element biproduct component, to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
805	Project-local. -/
806	lemma matrixEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
807	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
808	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
809	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom ≫
810	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
811	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
812	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
813	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q.inv`).
814	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
815	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin d))` agree on each
816	  -- free injection `(pullback p).map (ιFree i)` (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
817	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
818	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
819	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
820	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
821	  -- `Q.hom` is, by construction of `pullbackFreeIso`, the Mathlib free-pullback comparison.
822	  have hQhom : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
823	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl
824	  -- The free injection cancels against `Q.hom` into the unit-pullback comparison
825	  -- (`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`), which is where `scalarEnd_pullback` lives.
826	  have key : ∀ k : Fin d,
827	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
828	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
829	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
830	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
831	    intro k
832	    rw [hQhom]
833	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
834	  -- LHS: `map (ιFree i) ≫ map (matrixEnd M)` collapses to `map (ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M)`,
835	  -- then `ιFree_matrixEnd` turns it into a row sum, distributed by additivity of the pullback.
836	  rw [← Functor.map_comp_assoc]
837	  -- `erw` (defeq matching) is needed to see `ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M` under `(pullback p).map`.
838	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd M i]
839	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
840	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
841	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, then expand.
842	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
843	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom,
844	    key i]
845	  erw [Category.assoc]
846	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) i]
847	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
848	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
849	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
850	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
851	  rw [Category.assoc, key k]
852	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
853	  erw [Category.assoc]
854	
855	/-- The action of `matrixEndRect M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect M`
856	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
857	for `matrixEndRect_pullback` (rectangular analogue of `ιFree_matrixEnd`). -/
858	lemma ιFree_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤))
859	    (j : Fin r) :
860	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M
861	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
862	  rw [matrixEndRect, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
863	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
864	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
865	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
866	  rfl
867	
868	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
869	/-- **Rectangular matrix homomorphism naturality under pullback**
870	(`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback`). For `p : T ⟶ S` and a `d × r` matrix `M` of global
871	sections, the pullback of `matrixEndRect M` is, after the free-pullback comparisons
872	`Q_r = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)` and `Q_d = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`, the rectangular
873	homomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p^♯ M`:

text: Key discovery: `scalarEnd_pullback` already exists as a proven atom (GrassmannianQuot.lean:760). Let me examine `pullbackUnitIso`'s definition and check which rank-1 coherences already exist, while kicking off recon on Mathlib's Proj basic-open/away API (the crux for the geometric bridge).

1020	@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1021	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1022	    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom =
1023	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom := rfl
1024	
1025	/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
1026	`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X`, where
1027	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is
1028	always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
1029	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/
1030	noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
1031	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1032	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1033	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
1034	  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f
1035	
1036	/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
1037	sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
1038	presheaf tensor `P ⊗ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
1039	presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val ⊗ (a Q).val`, where
1040	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`. This is the "sheafification is
1041	monoidal" reconciliation, built — exactly as in `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — by
1042	whiskering the sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
1043	on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
1044	with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
1045	presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `⊗_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
1046	sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
1047	`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
1048	noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
1049	    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1050	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1051	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) P Q) ≅
1052	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1053	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)
1054	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1055	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
1056	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1057	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
1058	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
1059	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
1060	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1061	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
1062	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
1063	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
1064	  have hηP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W

399	/-- **Unit coherence (`map_comp` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`).** The composite
400	analogue of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`: the Mathlib free-pullback comparison at a composite
401	morphism `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`. Project-local.
402	
403	Transposing both sides under the composite pullback-pushforward adjunction: the LHS collapses
404	by `homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app` to `uTPU (b ≫ a) ≫ (conjugate of pullbackComp.hom)`, the
405	conjugate is `(pushforwardComp).inv` via `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` + `conjugateEquiv_comm`,
406	and the RHS collapses by `homEquiv` naturality to `uTPU a ≫ pushforward a (uTPU b)`;
407	both reduce to the unit-section identity (`pushforwardComp_inv_app_app = 𝟙`). -/
408	lemma gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
409	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
410	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))
411	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
412	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
413	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) := by
414	  -- Work with the Scheme-level adjunctions so `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` lines up.
415	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
416	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
417	  -- abbreviations
418	  set adjA := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a
419	  set adjB := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b
420	  set adjBA := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)
421	  -- LHS: collapse via the mate-compatibility helper (term-mode `.trans`, so `pullbackComp`
422	  -- stays OPAQUE and matching is up to defeq rather than syntactic `rw`).
423	  have hL := homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
424	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom
425	      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)))
426	  -- transpose of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` (used via defeq).
427	  have hL2 : adjBA.homEquiv _ _
428	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)))

332	    exact (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose_spec
333	
334	/-- `Proj A` is covered by `Spec (A_f)₀` for all homogeneous elements of positive degree. -/
335	@[simps! f] noncomputable
336	def affineOpenCover : (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover :=
337	  affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜
338	    (ι := Σ i : PNat, 𝒜 i) (m := fun i ↦ i.1) (fun i ↦ i.2) (fun i ↦ i.2.2) (fun i ↦ i.1.2) <| by
339	  classical
340	  intro z hz
341	  rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose 𝒜 z]
342	  refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun c hc ↦ if hc0 : c = 0 then ?_ else
343	    Ideal.subset_span ⟨⟨⟨c, Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hc0⟩, _⟩, rfl⟩
344	  convert! Ideal.zero_mem _
345	  subst hc0
346	  exact hz
347	
348	end basicOpen
349	
350	section stalk
351	
352	/-- The stalk of `Proj A` at `x` is the degree `0` part of the localization of `A` at `x`. -/
353	noncomputable
354	def stalkIso (x : Proj 𝒜) :
355	    (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.stalk x ≅ .of (AtPrime 𝒜 x.asHomogeneousIdeal.toIdeal) :=
356	  (stalkIso' 𝒜 x).toCommRingCatIso
357	
358	end stalk
359	
360	noncomputable section ofGlobalSection

598	section

600	/-- Localizing away from powers of `f` homogeneously. -/
601	abbrev Away (𝒜 : ι → σ) (f : A) :=
602	  HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 (Submonoid.powers f)
603	
604	variable [AddSubgroupClass σ A] [AddCommMonoid ι] [DecidableEq ι]
605	variable (𝒜 : ι → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] {f : A}
606	
607	/-- This is a convenient constructor for `Away 𝒜 f` when `f` is homogeneous.
608	`Away.mk 𝒜 hf n x hx` is the fraction `x / f ^ n`. -/
609	protected def Away.mk {d : ι} (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 d) (n : ℕ) (x : A) (hx : x ∈ 𝒜 (n • d)) : Away 𝒜 f :=
610	  HomogeneousLocalization.mk ⟨n • d, ⟨x, hx⟩, ⟨f ^ n, SetLike.pow_mem_graded n hf⟩, ⟨n, rfl⟩⟩
611	
612	@[simp]
613	lemma Away.val_mk {d : ι} (n : ℕ) (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 d) (x : A) (hx : x ∈ 𝒜 (n • d)) :
614	    (Away.mk 𝒜 hf n x hx).val = Localization.mk x ⟨f ^ n, by use n⟩ :=
615	  rfl
616	
617	protected
618	lemma Away.mk_surjective {d : ι} (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 d) (x : Away 𝒜 f) :
619	    ∃ n a ha, Away.mk 𝒜 hf n a ha = x := by
620	  obtain ⟨⟨N, ⟨s, hs⟩, ⟨b, hn⟩, ⟨n, (rfl : _ = b)⟩⟩, rfl⟩ := mk_surjective x
621	  by_cases hfn : f ^ n = 0
622	  · have := HomogeneousLocalization.subsingleton 𝒜 (x := .powers f) ⟨n, hfn⟩
623	    exact ⟨0, 0, zero_mem _, Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
624	  obtain rfl := DirectSum.degree_eq_of_mem_mem 𝒜 hn (SetLike.pow_mem_graded n hf) hfn
625	  exact ⟨n, s, hs, by ext; simp⟩
626	
627	variable {𝒜}

629	theorem Away.eventually_smul_mem {m} (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (z : Away 𝒜 f) :
630	    ∀ᶠ n in Filter.atTop, f ^ n • z.val ∈ algebraMap _ _ '' (𝒜 (n • m) : Set A) := by
631	  obtain ⟨k, hk : f ^ k = _⟩ := z.den_mem
632	  apply Filter.mem_of_superset (Filter.Ici_mem_atTop k)
633	  rintro k' (hk' : k ≤ k')
634	  simp only [Set.mem_image, SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_setOf_eq]

778	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
779	/-- Given `f ∣ x`, this is the map `A_{(f)} → A_f → A_x`. We will lift this to a map
780	`A_{(f)} → A_{(x)}` in `awayMap`. -/
781	private def awayMapAux (hx : f ∣ x) : Away 𝒜 f →+* Localization.Away x :=
782	  (Localization.awayLift (algebraMap A _) _
783	    (isUnit_of_dvd_unit (map_dvd _ hx) (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit x))).comp
784	      (algebraMap (Away 𝒜 f) (Localization.Away f))

789	    awayMapAux 𝒜 ⟨_, hx⟩ (mk ⟨n, a, ⟨f ^ i, hi⟩, ⟨i, rfl⟩⟩) =
790	      Localization.mk (a * g ^ i) ⟨x ^ i, (Submonoid.mem_powers_iff _ _).mpr ⟨i, rfl⟩⟩ := by
791	  have : algebraMap A (Localization.Away x) f *
792	    (Localization.mk g ⟨f * g, (Submonoid.mem_powers_iff _ _).mpr ⟨1, by simp [hx]⟩⟩) = 1 := by
793	    rw [← Algebra.smul_def, Localization.smul_mk]
794	    exact Localization.mk_self ⟨f*g, _⟩
795	  simp only [awayMapAux, RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, algebraMap_apply, val_mk]
796	  rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (hv := this), ← Algebra.smul_def,
797	    Localization.mk_pow, Localization.smul_mk]
798	  subst hx
799	  rfl

802	set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in

804	lemma range_awayMapAux_subset :
805	    Set.range (awayMapAux 𝒜 (f := f) ⟨_, hx⟩) ⊆ Set.range (val (𝒜 := 𝒜)) := by
806	  rintro _ ⟨z, rfl⟩
807	  obtain ⟨⟨n, ⟨a, ha⟩, ⟨b, hb'⟩, j, rfl : _ = b⟩, rfl⟩ := mk_surjective z
808	  use mk ⟨n+j•e,⟨a*g^j, ?_⟩, ⟨x^j, ?_⟩, j, rfl⟩
809	  · simp [awayMapAux_mk 𝒜 (hx := hx)]
810	  · apply SetLike.mul_mem_graded ha
811	    exact SetLike.pow_mem_graded _ hg
812	  · rw [hx, mul_pow]
813	    apply SetLike.mul_mem_graded hb'
814	    exact SetLike.pow_mem_graded _ hg
815	
816	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
817	set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
818	/-- Given `x = f * g` with `g` homogeneous of positive degree,
819	this is the map `A_{(f)} → A_{(x)}` taking `a/f^i` to `ag^i/(fg)^i`. -/
820	def awayMap : Away 𝒜 f →+* Away 𝒜 x := by
821	  let e := RingEquiv.ofLeftInverse (f := algebraMap (Away 𝒜 x) (Localization.Away x))
822	    (h := (val_injective _).hasLeftInverse.choose_spec)
823	  refine RingHom.comp (e.symm.toRingHom.comp (Subring.inclusion ?_))
824	    (awayMapAux 𝒜 (f := f) ⟨_, hx⟩).rangeRestrict
825	  exact range_awayMapAux_subset 𝒜 hg hx
826	
827	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
828	set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
829	lemma val_awayMap_eq_aux (a) : (awayMap 𝒜 hg hx a).val = awayMapAux 𝒜 ⟨_, hx⟩ a := by
830	  let e := RingEquiv.ofLeftInverse (f := algebraMap (Away 𝒜 x) (Localization.Away x))
831	    (h := (val_injective _).hasLeftInverse.choose_spec)
832	  dsimp [awayMap]
833	  convert_to! (e (e.symm ⟨awayMapAux 𝒜 (f := f) ⟨_, hx⟩ a,
834	    range_awayMapAux_subset 𝒜 hg hx ⟨_, rfl⟩⟩)).1 = _
835	  rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
836	
837	lemma val_awayMap (a) : (awayMap 𝒜 hg hx a).val = Localization.awayLift (algebraMap A _) _
838	    (isUnit_of_dvd_unit (map_dvd _ ⟨_, hx⟩) (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit x)) a.val := by
839	  rw [val_awayMap_eq_aux]
840	  rfl

585	namespace ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj
586	
587	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
588	/--
589	The ring map from `A⁰_ f` to the local sections of the structure sheaf of the projective spectrum of
590	`A` on the basic open set `D(f)` defined by sending `s ∈ A⁰_f` to the section `x ↦ s` on `D(f)`.
591	-/
592	def awayToSection (f) : CommRingCat.of (A⁰_ f) ⟶ (structureSheaf 𝒜).1.obj (op (pbo f)) :=
593	  CommRingCat.ofHom
594	    -- Have to hint `S`, otherwise it gets unfolded to `structureSheafInType`
595	    -- causing `ext` to fail
596	    (S := (structureSheaf 𝒜).1.obj (op (pbo f)))
597	  { toFun s :=
598	      ⟨fun x ↦ HomogeneousLocalization.mapId 𝒜 (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr x.2) s, fun x ↦ by
599	        obtain ⟨s, rfl⟩ := HomogeneousLocalization.mk_surjective s
600	        obtain ⟨n, hn : f ^ n = s.den.1⟩ := s.den_mem
601	        exact ⟨_, x.2, 𝟙 _, s.1, s.2, s.3,
602	          fun x hsx ↦ x.2 (Ideal.IsPrime.mem_of_pow_mem inferInstance n (hn ▸ hsx)), fun _ ↦ rfl⟩⟩
603	    map_add' _ _ := by ext; simp only [map_add, HomogeneousLocalization.val_add, Proj.add_apply]
604	    map_mul' _ _ := by ext; simp only [map_mul, HomogeneousLocalization.val_mul, Proj.mul_apply]
605	    map_zero' := by ext; simp only [map_zero, HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero, Proj.zero_apply]
606	    map_one' := by ext; simp only [map_one, HomogeneousLocalization.val_one, Proj.one_apply] }
607	
608	lemma awayToSection_germ (f x hx) :
609	    awayToSection 𝒜 f ≫ (structureSheaf 𝒜).presheaf.germ _ x hx =
610	      CommRingCat.ofHom (HomogeneousLocalization.mapId 𝒜 (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr hx)) ≫
611	        (Proj.stalkIso' 𝒜 x).toCommRingCatIso.inv := by
612	  ext z
613	  apply (Proj.stalkIso' 𝒜 x).eq_symm_apply.mpr
614	  apply Proj.stalkIso'_germ
615	
616	lemma awayToSection_apply (f : A) (x p) :
617	    (((ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f).1 x).val p).val =
618	      IsLocalization.map (M := Submonoid.powers f) (T := p.1.1.toIdeal.primeCompl) _
619	        (RingHom.id _) (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr p.2) x.val := by
620	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := HomogeneousLocalization.mk_surjective x
621	  change (HomogeneousLocalization.mapId 𝒜 _ _).val = _
622	  dsimp [HomogeneousLocalization.mapId, HomogeneousLocalization.map]
623	  rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk', Localization.mk_eq_mk', IsLocalization.map_mk']
624	  rfl
625	
626	/--
627	The ring map from `A⁰_ f` to the global sections of the structure sheaf of the projective spectrum
628	of `A` restricted to the basic open set `D(f)`.
629	
630	Mathematically, the map is the same as `awayToSection`.
631	-/
632	def awayToΓ (f) : CommRingCat.of (A⁰_ f) ⟶ LocallyRingedSpace.Γ.obj (op <| Proj| pbo f) :=

text: Now let me read the `mapId` definition and search the project itself for existing usages.

data.content:
660	    (hw : W₁ ≤ W₂.comap f) (c : NumDenSameDeg 𝒜 W₁) : NumDenSameDeg ℬ W₂ where
661	  deg := c.deg
662	  den := f.gradedAddHom _ c.den
663	  num := f.gradedAddHom _ c.num
664	  den_mem := hw c.den_mem
665	
666	/--
667	Let `A, B` be two graded rings with the same indexing set and `g : 𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ` be a graded ring
668	homomorphism. Let `P ≤ A` be a submonoid and `Q ≤ B` be a submonoid such that `P ≤ g⁻¹ Q`, then `g`
669	induces a map from the homogeneous localization `A⁰_P` to the homogeneous localization `B⁰_Q`.
670	-/
671	def map (g : 𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ) (comap_le : P ≤ Q.comap g) :
672	    HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 P →+* HomogeneousLocalization ℬ Q where
673	  toFun := Quotient.map'
674	    (fun x ↦ ⟨x.1, ⟨_, map_mem g x.2.2⟩, ⟨_, map_mem g x.3.2⟩, comap_le x.4⟩)
675	    fun x y (e : x.embedding = y.embedding) ↦ by
676	      apply_fun IsLocalization.map (Localization Q) g.toRingHom comap_le at e
677	      simp_rw [HomogeneousLocalization.NumDenSameDeg.embedding, Localization.mk_eq_mk',
678	        IsLocalization.map_mk', ← Localization.mk_eq_mk'] at e
679	      exact e
680	  map_add' := Quotient.ind₂' fun x y ↦ by
681	    simp only [← mk_add, Quotient.map'_mk'', num_add, map_add, map_mul, den_add]; rfl
682	  map_mul' := Quotient.ind₂' fun x y ↦ by
683	    simp only [← mk_mul, Quotient.map'_mk'', num_mul, map_mul, den_mul]; rfl
684	  map_zero' := by simp only [← mk_zero (𝒜 := 𝒜), Quotient.map'_mk'', deg_zero,
685	    num_zero, ZeroMemClass.coe_zero, map_zero, den_zero, map_one]; rfl
686	  map_one' := by simp only [← mk_one (𝒜 := 𝒜), Quotient.map'_mk'',
687	    num_one, den_one, map_one]; rfl
688	
689	variable (𝒜) in
690	/--
691	Let `A` be a graded ring and `P ≤ Q` be two submonoids, then the homogeneous localization of `A`
692	at `P` embeds into the homogeneous localization of `A` at `Q`.
693	-/
694	abbrev mapId {P Q : Submonoid A} (h : P ≤ Q) :
695	    HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 P →+* HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 Q :=
696	  map (.id _) h
697	
698	lemma map_mk (g : 𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ) (comap_le : P ≤ Q.comap g) (x) :
699	    map g comap_le (mk x) = mk ⟨x.1, ⟨_, map_mem g x.2.2⟩, ⟨_, map_mem g x.3.2⟩, comap_le x.4⟩ :=
700	  rfl
701	
702	variable (𝒜) in
703	@[simp] theorem map_id (P : Submonoid A) : map (.id 𝒜) (P := P) (Q := P) le_rfl = .id _ := by
704	  ext x

150	open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ) = D₊(Xᵢ) ⊓ D₊(Xⱼ)`. -/
151	lemma range_overlap_le (i j : n) :
152	    Set.range (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
153	        (basicOpenCover n).f i).base ⊆
154	      Set.range ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
155	        (X i * X j)).ι).base := by

157	  rintro _ ⟨v, rfl⟩
158	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
159	  · have hx : ((pullback.fst _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f i) v : Proj _) ∈
160	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
161	          (X i)).ι) := by
162	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
163	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
164	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
165	  · have hcond := congrArg (fun (φ : pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i)
166	        ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶ Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) =>
167	          φ.base v) pullback.condition

208	def unitScalarIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} (v : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) :

210	  hom := scalarEnd v.val

224	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso φ ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ψ).symm

237	  rw [hden]

249	def twistTransition (m : ℕ) (i j : n) :

251	        (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf) ≅

253	        (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U j).ringCatSheaf) :=
254	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫
255	    unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i j) ^ m) ≪≫
256	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm
257

259	cast: `Xᵢ/Xᵢ = 1`, so the scalar automorphism is the identity and the two

262	    twistTransition n m i i
263	      = eqToIso (congrArg
264	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
265	            (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf))
266	          (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
267	            rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])) := by
268	  have hmid : unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i i) ^ m)
269	      = Iso.refl (SheafOfModules.unit
270	          (pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f i)).ringCatSheaf) := by

274	          unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i i) ^ m) ≪≫
275	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso

277	    _ = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫
278	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
279	            ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm := by
280	        refine (congrArg (fun e =>
281	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫ e ≪≫
282	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
283	              ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm) hmid).trans ?_
284	        exact congrArg (fun e => Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫ e)
285	          (Iso.refl_trans _)
286	    _ = eqToIso (congrArg
287	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
288	            (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf))
289	          (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by

291	      pullbackUnitIso_trans_symm_eqToIso
292	        (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by

# Mathlib `Proj 𝒜` (ℕ-graded) structure-sheaf / basic-open API

Two files carry the API. Paths below are the project's copy under `.lake-packages/`; the `.shared-packages/` copy is byte-identical modulo a few line offsets.

- `…/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean` — namespace `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj`, the **user-facing** graded-`ℕ` API. Ambient context (lines 44–47): `{σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A] (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜]`. This is exactly your `𝒜 : ℕ → Submodule …` case.
- `…/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean` — namespace `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj`, the **underlying/internal** construction (notation `A⁰_ f := Away 𝒜 f`, `pbo f := ProjectiveSpectrum.basicOpen 𝒜 f`, `Proj| pbo f`).
- `…/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Topology.lean` — `ProjectiveSpectrum.basicOpen` and its lattice lemmas.
- `…/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean` — `Away`, `awayMap`, `mapId`.

**IMPORTANT naming caveat:** there are TWO `awayToSection`s. `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayToSection` (Basic.lean:135) is the wrapper you want; `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj.awayToSection` (Scheme.lean:592) is the internal definition it delegates to. Likewise `Proj.basicOpen` (Basic) vs `ProjectiveSpectrum.basicOpen` (Topology).

---

## 1. `Proj.basicOpen` and its lattice API

All in `Basic.lean`, namespace `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj`, with `(f g : A)` explicit:

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpen`** — Basic.lean:54
  `def basicOpen (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) (f : A) : (Proj 𝒜).Opens := ProjectiveSpectrum.basicOpen 𝒜 f`
  D₊(f) as an open of the scheme `Proj 𝒜`.

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpen_mul`** — Basic.lean:69 ✅ EXISTS
  `theorem basicOpen_mul : basicOpen 𝒜 (f * g) = basicOpen 𝒜 f ⊓ basicOpen 𝒜 g`
  D₊(fg) = D₊(f) ⊓ D₊(g). (delegates to `ProjectiveSpectrum.basicOpen_mul`.)

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpen_mono`** — Basic.lean:72 ✅ EXISTS (this is your "D₊(fg) ≤ D₊(f)" via divisibility)
  `theorem basicOpen_mono (hfg : f ∣ g) : basicOpen 𝒜 g ≤ basicOpen 𝒜 f`
  Divisibility ⇒ reverse ⊆ on basic opens. Used throughout as `basicOpen_mono 𝒜 f x ⟨g, hx⟩ : basicOpen 𝒜 x ≤ basicOpen 𝒜 f` when `hx : x = f * g`.

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpen_pow`** — Basic.lean:66 ✅ EXISTS
  `@[simp] theorem basicOpen_pow (n) (hn : 0 < n) : basicOpen 𝒜 (f ^ n) = basicOpen 𝒜 f`

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpen_one`** — Basic.lean:62: `basicOpen 𝒜 1 = ⊤`.

## 2. `Proj.awayToSection`

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayToSection`** — Basic.lean:135
  `def awayToSection (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) (f : A) : CommRingCat.of (Away 𝒜 f) ⟶ Γ(Proj 𝒜, basicOpen 𝒜 f)`
  It is a **`CommRingCat` morphism** (`⟶`), i.e. a bundled ring hom in `CommRingCat`; `.hom` gives the underlying `RingHom`. Maps the degree-0 away localization `A⁰_f = Away 𝒜 f` into sections over D₊(f). Delegates to the internal one.

- Internal: **`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj.awayToSection`** — Scheme.lean:592
  `def awayToSection (f) : CommRingCat.of (A⁰_ f) ⟶ (structureSheaf 𝒜).1.obj (op (pbo f))`
  Built as `CommRingCat.ofHom { toFun s := ⟨fun x ↦ HomogeneousLocalization.mapId 𝒜 (…) s, …⟩, map_add', map_mul', map_zero', map_one' }` — a genuine bundled ring hom (all four ring-hom fields discharged).
  - `awayToSection_apply` — Scheme.lean:616 (value formula via `IsLocalization.map`).
  - `awayToSection_germ` — Scheme.lean:608 (compatibility with germs/`stalkIso'`).

Related maps:
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj.awayToΓ`** — Scheme.lean:632
  `def awayToΓ (f) : CommRingCat.of (A⁰_ f) ⟶ LocallyRingedSpace.Γ.obj (op <| Proj| pbo f)` = `awayToSection ≫ presheaf.map (homOfLE …).op`.
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpenToSpec`** — Basic.lean:141
  `def basicOpenToSpec (f) : (basicOpen 𝒜 f).toScheme ⟶ Spec (.of <| Away 𝒜 f)` = `(basicOpen 𝒜 f).toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (awayToSection 𝒜 f)`.

---

## 3. ★ THE KEY QUESTION — restriction naturality of `awayToSection` across `A_f → A_{fg}` ★

**YES, it exists, exactly in the shape you asked for.**

**`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayMap_awayToSection`** — Basic.lean:222, `@[reassoc]`

Ambient (Basic.lean:218–219): `variable {f}` then `{m' : ℕ} {g : A} (g_deg : g ∈ 𝒜 m') (hm' : 0 < m') {x : A} (hx : x = f * g)`. Effective signature (only `g_deg` and `hx` are actually needed):

```
theorem awayMap_awayToSection
    {m' : ℕ} {f g x : A} (g_deg : g ∈ 𝒜 m') (hx : x = f * g) :
    CommRingCat.ofHom (HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx) ≫ awayToSection 𝒜 x
      = awayToSection 𝒜 f
          ≫ (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.map (homOfLE (basicOpen_mono 𝒜 f x ⟨g, hx⟩)).op
```

Read this literally: pushing a degree-0 fraction from `A⁰_f` into `A⁰_x = A⁰_{fg}` via `HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap` and then taking its section over D₊(fg) **equals** taking its section over D₊(f) and then restricting along the presheaf restriction map for the inclusion `D₊(fg) ≤ D₊(f)`. This is precisely "`awayToSection f x` restricted to D₊(fg) = `awayToSection (fg) (image of x)`". Being `@[reassoc]`, there is also `awayMap_awayToSection_assoc`.

Downstream naturality lemmas built on it (all Basic.lean, all `@[reassoc]`):
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpenToSpec_SpecMap_awayMap`** — Basic.lean:240
  `basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 x ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx)) = (Proj 𝒜).homOfLE (basicOpen_mono 𝒜 f x ⟨g, hx⟩) ≫ basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 f` — the Spec-side square.
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι`** — Basic.lean:248
  `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx)) ≫ awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm = awayι 𝒜 x (…) (…)` — the open-immersion factorization D₊(fg) → D₊(f) → Proj.

No other spelling ("`awayToΓ`/`toOpen`/`awayι` restriction to a smaller basic open") exists separately — `awayMap_awayToSection` is THE lemma, and everything else is derived from it.

---

## 4. The iso `D₊(f) ≅ Spec (A_f)` and its naturality

All in `Basic.lean`, requiring `f` homogeneous of positive degree: `variable {m} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m)` (Basic.lean:155).

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec`** — Basic.lean:161, `@[simps! -isSimp hom]`

  Direction: **D₊(f) ⟶ Spec (A_f)** on `.hom` (= `basicOpenToSpec 𝒜 f`, via `basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`). This is THE `D₊(f) ≅ Spec A_f`.
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpenIsoAway`** — Basic.lean:176, `@[simps! -isSimp hom]`
  `CommRingCat.of (Away 𝒜 f) ≅ Γ(Proj 𝒜, basicOpen 𝒜 f)` — ring-level iso (`asIso (awayToSection 𝒜 f)`), i.e. `awayToSection` is an iso for homogeneous positive-degree `f`.
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι`** — Basic.lean:186
  `noncomputable def awayι (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) : Spec (.of <| Away 𝒜 f) ⟶ Proj 𝒜` = `(basicOpenIsoSpec …).inv ≫ (basicOpen 𝒜 f).ι`. The open immersion Spec(A_f) → Proj. `instance : IsOpenImmersion (awayι …)` at Basic.lean:193; `opensRange_awayι` (:196) says its range is `basicOpen 𝒜 f`.

**Naturality of this iso w.r.t. inclusion of basic opens — YES, and additionally the pullback identification:**
- `basicOpenToSpec_SpecMap_awayMap` (Basic.lean:240) and `SpecMap_awayMap_awayι` (Basic.lean:248), above.
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`** — Basic.lean:256

  — "D₊(f) ×[Proj 𝒜] D₊(g) ≅ D₊(fg)". This is the **two-fold-overlap identification** for the Proj basic-open cover.
  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι` (:265), `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left/right` (:272/:279), `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst/snd` (:289/:295) — all `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]`, expressing the fst/snd legs as `Spec.map (awayMap …)`.
- `awayι_preimage_basicOpen` — Basic.lean:301: `awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ basicOpen 𝒜 g = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (Away.isLocalizationElem f_deg g_deg)`.

Internal Scheme.lean analogues (over `A⁰_ f`, not needed if you use the `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj` layer): `ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj.toSpec` (Scheme.lean:653, `(Proj| pbo f) ⟶ Spec (A⁰_ f)`), `isIso_toSpec` (:818), `projIsoSpec` (:835). Note: there is **no** `Proj.awayToSpec`, `Proj.awayιIso`, `Proj.toSpec`, or `Proj.awayIso` under the `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj` namespace — the names are exactly `basicOpenToSpec`, `basicOpenIsoSpec`, `basicOpenIsoAway`, `awayι`.

---

## 5. `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` and the map `A_f → A_{fg}`

`HomogeneousLocalization.lean`, ambient `{ι σ A}` graded (`[AddSubgroupClass σ A] [AddCommMonoid ι] [DecidableEq ι] (𝒜 : ι → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜]`):

- **`HomogeneousLocalization.Away`** — line 601
  `abbrev Away (𝒜 : ι → σ) (f : A) := HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 (Submonoid.powers f)`
  The degree-0 away localization `A⁰_f`.
  - `Away.mk 𝒜 hf n x hx` — line 609 (the fraction `x / fⁿ`); `Away.val_mk` (:613); `Away.mk_surjective` (:618).

- **`HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap`** — line 820 ← THE canonical `A_f → A_{fg}` map
  Ambient (line 776): `{e : ι} {f g : A} (hg : g ∈ 𝒜 e) {x : A} (hx : x = f * g)`.
  `def awayMap (𝒜 : ι → σ) (hg : g ∈ 𝒜 e) (hx : x = f * g) : Away 𝒜 f →+* Away 𝒜 x`
  A `RingHom`, "taking `a/fⁱ ↦ a·gⁱ/(fg)ⁱ`". Requires `g` homogeneous (`hg`) and `x = f*g` (`hx`).
  - `awayMap_mk` — line 863 (`@[simp]`): `awayMap 𝒜 hg hx (Away.mk 𝒜 hf n a ha) = Away.mk 𝒜 (hx ▸ mul_mem_graded hf hg) n (a*gⁿ) (…)`.
  - `val_awayMap` — line 837; `val_awayMap_mk` — line 849 (`… = Localization.mk (a*gⁱ) ⟨xⁱ, …⟩`).
  - `awayMap_fromZeroRingHom` — line 842 (commutes with `𝒜 0`).
  - **`HomogeneousLocalization.awayMapₐ`** — line 855: the same map as an `𝒜 0`-algebra hom `Away 𝒜 f →ₐ[𝒜 0] Away 𝒜 x` (`awayMapₐ_apply` :859).
  - `Away.isLocalization_mul` — line 883: with `awayMap`'s algebra structure, `Away 𝒜 x` is the localization of `Away 𝒜 f` away from `Away.isLocalizationElem` (line 877). This is what makes `awayMap` a genuine localization inclusion.

- Also (general functoriality, different signature): **`HomogeneousLocalization.mapId`** — line 694
  `abbrev mapId (𝒜) {P Q : Submonoid A} (h : P ≤ Q) : HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 P →+* HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 Q := map (.id _) h`
  (`map` at line 671). `awayToSection` is defined pointwise via `mapId 𝒜 (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr x.2)`.
  There is also `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.map` (line 724, functoriality along a graded ring hom `g : 𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ`) — a *different* map (`Away 𝒜 f →+* Away ℬ (g f)`), not the localization-inclusion you want.

There is **no** `HomogeneousLocalization.mapAway`; the name is `awayMap` (ring) / `awayMapₐ` (algebra).

---

## 6. Does `awayToSection` respect `*`, `1`, `^`?

**Yes.** `ProjectiveSpectrum.Proj.awayToSection` (Scheme.lean:592) is `CommRingCat.ofHom` of a bundled `RingHom` whose `map_mul'` (:604), `map_one'` (:606), `map_add'` (:603), `map_zero'` (:605) are all provided; hence it preserves `+, *, 0, 1` and therefore `^` (and `Units.map` applies, as the project does). The wrapper `Proj.awayToSection` (Basic.lean:135) inherits this. The presheaf restriction `(Proj 𝒜).presheaf.map _` is a `CommRingCat` morphism (ring hom) too, so the composite in `awayMap_awayToSection` is a ring hom on both sides — products and powers are preserved. There is no single named "`awayToSection_pow`"; it follows from `map_pow`/`map_mul` applied to `(awayToSection …).hom`.

---

## Project-side findings (grep under `AlgebraicJacobian/`)

- **The project already uses `Proj.awayToSection` and builds an overlap ring hom** — `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SerreTwist.lean`:
  - `Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift ℤ)) (X i * X j)` at SerreTwist.lean:196, inside
  - **`SerreTwist.overlapRingHom`** — SerreTwist.lean:190
    `def overlapRingHom (i j : n) : Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift ℤ)) (X i * X j) →+* Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤)`
    = `(overlapHom i j).appTop.hom.comp ((Proj.basicOpen … (X i*X j)).topIso.inv.hom).comp (Proj.awayToSection … (X i*X j)).hom`.
    This is the project's existing bridge from the degree-0 away ring `A⁰_{XᵢXⱼ}` to sections on the scheme-theoretic overlap — but it routes through `overlapHom` (an `IsOpenImmersion.lift` into `D₊(XᵢXⱼ)`, SerreTwist.lean:179) and `.appTop`, NOT through `awayMap`/`awayMap_awayToSection`. `overlapUnit` (:200), `overlapUnit_self` (:243), `twistTransition` (:249), `twistTransition_self` (:261, "C1"), `twistTransition_cocycle` (:302, "C2") sit on top.
  - `Proj.basicOpen_mul` is used at SerreTwist.lean:156 (`range_overlap_le`).
- `Proj.basicOpen` cover machinery: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SerreTwist.lean:71,96–101,154–171` (`basicOpenCover`).
- **No project use of `awayMap_awayToSection`, `HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap`, `Proj.awayι`, `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`, `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec`, or `Proj.basicOpenToSpec` was found.** The `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`/`specBasicOpen` occurrences in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/*` (CechTermAcyclic, AffineSerreVanishing, QcohRestrictBasicOpen, QcohTildeSections) and the many `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul` uses are for the **affine `Spec` / `PrimeSpectrum`** basic-open theory, NOT the `Proj` graded theory — different API, do not confuse them.
- **Triple-overlap helpers in the project are purely ring-theoretic (Grassmannian), not Proj-sheaf.** `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean` (lines 292–1110) has `triple-overlap transition maps` `Θ_{I,J}`, cocycle `Φ = id`, etc., over localized matrix rings — unrelated to `awayToSection`. `SerreTwist.twistTransition_cocycle` (the Proj "C2") does not currently use any `awayMap`-based triple-overlap identification; it works at the level of `overlapUnit`/`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`.

**Bottom line for a Proj basic-open cover restriction/triple-overlap identification:** the ready-made Mathlib building block is `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayMap_awayToSection` (Basic.lean:222) together with `HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap` (HomogeneousLocalization.lean:820) and `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (Basic.lean:256); the project has not yet wired these in (it uses the `overlapHom`/`appTop` route in SerreTwist instead).

universe u

/-! ## Rank-one pullback coherences

The Serre-twist cocycle transports rank-one transition isomorphisms of the shape
`pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).symm` to a triple
overlap.  The next block is the rank-one mirror of the free-sheaf coherence stack
of `GrassmannianQuot.lean` / `GlueDescent.lean` (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`,
`matrixEnd_pullback`, the `pullbackCongr` endpoint absorptions): with
`SheafOfModules.unit` in place of `SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)`, `scalarEnd` in
place of `matrixEnd`, and `pullbackUnitIso` in place of `pullbackFreeIso`.  Each
is immediate from an already-proven unit-level atom. -/

namespace Scheme.Modules

open AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian (scalarEnd)

/-- Rank-one `map_comp` coherence (analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_comp`): the
structure-sheaf pullback comparison at a composite `b ≫ a` factors through the
pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`.  Immediate from the unit coherence
`gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (`(pullbackUnitIso f).hom = pullbackObjUnitToUnit f`). -/
lemma pullbackUnitIso_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
    (pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
        (pullbackUnitIso (b ≫ a)).hom
      = (pullback b).map (pullbackUnitIso a).hom ≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).hom :=
  gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp a b

/-- Iso-conjugated form of the scalar-endomorphism pullback atom `scalarEnd_pullback`:
`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) = Q.hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) ≫ Q.inv`, with
`Q = pullbackUnitIso p`.  The rank-one analogue of `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
lemma scalarEnd_pullback_iso {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
    (pullback p).map (scalarEnd a)
      = (pullbackUnitIso p).hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) ≫ (pullbackUnitIso p).inv := by
  have key : (pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ (pullbackUnitIso p).hom
      = (pullbackUnitIso p).hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) :=
    scalarEnd_pullback p a
  rw [← Category.assoc, ← key, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]

/-- Closed zig-zag: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = 𝟙` for equal base
morphisms `φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr_hom`. -/
@[reassoc]
lemma pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr_hom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
    (pullbackUnitIso φ).inv ≫
        ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
        (pullbackUnitIso ψ).hom
      = 𝟙 _ := by
  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]

/-- Left absorption: `pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = Q_φ` for equal base morphisms
`φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackCongr_hom_app_free`. -/
@[reassoc]
lemma pullbackCongr_hom_app_unit {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
    ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
        (pullbackUnitIso ψ).hom
      = (pullbackUnitIso φ).hom := by
  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]

/-- Right absorption: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app = Q_ψ⁻¹` for equal base
morphisms `φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr`. -/
@[reassoc]
lemma pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
    (pullbackUnitIso φ).inv ≫
        ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom
      = (pullbackUnitIso ψ).inv := by
  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]

end Scheme.Modules

/-- **Transport of a rank-one scalar automorphism through
`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the reusable abstract core of the Serre-twist
cocycle, the rank-one analogue of `pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso`
(`GrassmannianQuot.lean`).  A transition isomorphism of the twist shape
`pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).symm` (multiplication
by a unit `v` of `Γ(V, ⊤)`, conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along
`p : W ⟶ V` to the same shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, the scalar base-changed by
the comorphism `p.appTop`.  Combines the scalar-naturality atom
`scalarEnd_pullback_iso` with the pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackUnitIso_comp`. -/
lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) (v : Γ(V, ⊤)ˣ) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b).symm)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ a)).hom ≫
        scalarEnd (p.appTop v.val) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)).inv := by
  simp only [Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
    Iso.symm_hom, unitScalarIso_hom]
  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the
  -- composite comparison `Q_{p≫a}` (pseudofunctoriality, `pullbackUnitIso_comp`).
  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
          (SheafOfModules.unit Yi.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso a).hom ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ a)).hom := by
    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_comp a p]
    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
    rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]
  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into
  -- the inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`.
  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p).inv ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b).inv ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.unit Yj.ringCatSheaf)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)).inv := by
    have hiso : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.unit Yj.ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)
        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b) ≪≫
          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p := by
      apply Iso.ext
      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_comp b p
    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv
    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]
    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
    rw [Category.comp_id]
  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated scalar automorphism and apply the atom.
  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, Scheme.Modules.scalarEnd_pullback_iso]
  rw [← hfront, ← hback]
  rfl

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=

    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))

      intro Y Z f
      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
      congr 1)

variable (n₀ : Type)

/-- **Base cocycle of the transported overlap units** (`m = 1`): in
`Γ(V(i,j,k), O)`, the images of the three transition units `Xᵢ/Xⱼ`, `Xⱼ/Xₖ`,
`Xᵢ/Xₖ` under the base-change comorphisms to the common triple overlap satisfy
`p_IJ^♯(Xᵢ/Xⱼ) · p_JK^♯(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = p_IK^♯(Xᵢ/Xₖ)`.  This is the geometric heart of
the Serre-twist cocycle: it factors all three units through a common map
`V(i,j,k) ⟶ D₊(XᵢXⱼXₖ)` and reduces to the fraction identity
`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = Xᵢ/Xₖ` in `A⁰_{XᵢXⱼXₖ}` via `Proj.awayMap_awayToSection`. -/
lemma overlapUnit_cocycle_transport (i j k : n₀) :
    (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
        (overlapUnit n₀ i j).val *
      (Scheme.Hom.appTop ((glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫
          pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).f j i)))
        (overlapUnit n₀ j k).val
    = (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.snd ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
        (overlapUnit n₀ i k).val := by
  sorry

(`(Aᵐ)(Bᵐ) = (AB)ᵐ`). -/

      (Scheme.Hom.appTop ((glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫

`overlapUnit_cocycle_transport (i j k : n₀)` (with `n₀ : Type` from the surrounding `section Universe0`) states, writing `𝒜 := homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)` and `p^♯` for `Scheme.Hom.appTop`:

  (fst)^♯ (overlapUnit n₀ i j).val  *  (t'ijk ≫ fst_jk)^♯ (overlapUnit n₀ j k).val
    = (snd)^♯ (overlapUnit n₀ i k).val

in `Γ(V(i,j,k), ⊤)`, where V(i,j,k) = `pullback ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)`, `fst = pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)`, `snd = pullback.snd (...)`, and the middle map is `(glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).f j i)`.

## Key definitions (all in SerreTwist.lean; read them)
- `glueData n₀ = (basicOpenCover n₀).gluedCover` (Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.OpenCover.gluedCover`). So `(glueData n₀).J = n₀`, `(glueData n₀).U i = (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X i)).toScheme`, `(glueData n₀).V (i,j) = pullback (F i) (F j)` with `F i := (basicOpenCover n₀).f i = (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X i)).ι`, and `(glueData n₀).f i j = pullback.fst (F i) (F j)`. Investigate the exact `gluedCover` field defs in Mathlib (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Gluing.lean` / `Cover/`): the maps `.t i j`, `.t' i j k`, and the characterising simp lemmas (`gluedCover_t`, `pullbackSymmetry`, `.t_fac`, `.cocycle`, and how `.t i j ≫ pullback.fst = pullback.snd` etc.). These are essential.
- `overlapUnit n₀ i j = Units.map (overlapRingHom n₀ i j).toMonoidHom (awayUnit n₀ i j)`, so `(overlapUnit n₀ i j).val = overlapRingHom n₀ i j (awayFraction n₀ i j)`.
- `overlapRingHom n₀ i j = (overlapHom n₀ i j).appTop.hom ∘ (basicOpen 𝒜 (X i * X j)).topIso.inv.hom ∘ (Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 (X i * X j)).hom`  (a `RingHom` from `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i*X j)` to `Γ(V(i,j),⊤)`).
- `overlapHom n₀ i j := IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X i*X j)).ι (pullback.fst (F i)(F j) ≫ F i) (range_overlap_le ..)`. Its defining property (via `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac`): `overlapHom n₀ i j ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X i*X j)).ι = pullback.fst (F i)(F j) ≫ F i`.
- `awayFraction n₀ i j = Away.mk 𝒜 (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n₀ i j) 1 (X i * X i) _`  (= Xᵢ²/(XᵢXⱼ), i.e. Xᵢ/Xⱼ). Also present: `awayFraction_mul_inv`, `awayFraction_self` — study these for the algebra style.

## Proven Mathlib API you MUST use (in `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj`, file `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean`)
- `Proj.basicOpen_mul : basicOpen 𝒜 (f*g) = basicOpen 𝒜 f ⊓ basicOpen 𝒜 g`.
- `Proj.basicOpen_mono (hfg : f ∣ g) : basicOpen 𝒜 g ≤ basicOpen 𝒜 f`.
- `Proj.awayMap_awayToSection {m' : ℕ} {f g x : A} (g_deg : g ∈ 𝒜 m') (hx : x = f * g) : CommRingCat.ofHom (HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx) ≫ awayToSection 𝒜 x = awayToSection 𝒜 f ≫ (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.map (homOfLE (basicOpen_mono 𝒜 f x ⟨g, hx⟩)).op`  — THE restriction-naturality lemma (`@[reassoc]`).
- `HomogeneousLocalization.awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx : Away 𝒜 f →+* Away 𝒜 x` with `awayMap_mk`, `val_awayMap_mk`.
- `Proj.awayToSection 𝒜 f : CommRingCat.of (Away 𝒜 f) ⟶ Γ(Proj 𝒜, basicOpen 𝒜 f)` (a `CommRingCat` hom; `.hom` is the RingHom, preserves *, 1, ^).

## Recommended proof strategy (a rank-1 analogue of GrassmannianQuot's `baseChange_bridge`)
1. Fix `t : MvPolynomial n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ) := X i * X j * X k`, homogeneous of degree 3 (`X_mem_deg_one` gives deg-1 membership; take products).

Its defining property: `w ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 t).ι = struct`.
3. Prove the three factorisations by cancelling the relevant `.ι` (a mono, since it is an open immersion; use `Scheme.Hom.ι`-mono / `cancel_mono` or `IsOpenImmersion` mono):
   - `fst ≫ overlapHom n₀ i j = w ≫ (Proj 𝒜).homOfLE (h_ij)` with `h_ij : basicOpen 𝒜 t ≤ basicOpen 𝒜 (X i*X j)`.  Cancel `(basicOpen 𝒜 (X i*X j)).ι`: LHS∘ι = `fst ≫ pullback.fst(F i)(F j) ≫ F i = struct`; RHS∘ι = `w ≫ homOfLE(h_ij) ≫ ι_{XiXj} = w ≫ ι_t = struct` (using `Scheme.homOfLE_ι : homOfLE h ≫ U.ι = W.ι`).
   - `snd ≫ overlapHom n₀ i k = w ≫ homOfLE (h_ik)`: LHS∘ι = `snd ≫ pullback.fst(F i)(F k) ≫ F i`, and by `pullback.condition` for V(i,j,k) (`fst ≫ (glueData).f i j = snd ≫ (glueData).f i k`, i.e. `fst ≫ pullback.fst(F i)(F j) = snd ≫ pullback.fst(F i)(F k)`) this equals `struct`.
   - `(t'ijk ≫ pullback.fst((glueData).f j k)((glueData).f j i)) ≫ overlapHom n₀ j k = w ≫ homOfLE (h_jk)`: LHS∘ι = `p_JK ≫ pullback.fst(F j)(F k) ≫ F j`. Use `glueData_bridge_mid` (`fst ≫ ((glueData).t i j ≫ (glueData).f j i) = p_JK ≫ (glueData).f j k`) plus the `gluedCover.t` symmetry (`(glueData).t i j ≫ pullback.fst(F j)(F i) = pullback.snd(F i)(F j)`) plus `pullback.condition` to reduce it to `struct`. This is the hardest factorisation — expect to discover the exact `gluedCover_t`/`pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst` lemma names.

Find/derive the Mathlib lemma (`Scheme.Opens.topIso`, `Scheme.homOfLE_appTop`, `Scheme.Opens.ι_appTop`, or via `morphismRestrict`/`Scheme.Hom.appLE`). If no single lemma exists, prove it as a local `have`.
5. Combine 3+4+`awayMap_awayToSection` to rewrite each transported unit `p^♯ (overlapUnit ..).val` into `Φ (awayMap.. (awayFraction ..))` where `Φ := w.appTop ∘ (basicOpen 𝒜 t).topIso.inv ∘ awayToSection 𝒜 t` is a single ring hom into `Γ(V(i,j,k),⊤)`. Concretely: `(fst)^♯ (overlapRingHom i j (awayFraction i j)) = w^♯ ((basicOpen 𝒜 t).topIso.inv (awayToSection 𝒜 t (awayMap 𝒜 (X k deg) (hx : t = (X i*X j)*X k) (awayFraction i j))))`. Contravariance of `appTop`: `(a ≫ b)^♯ = b^♯ ≫ a^♯`.
6. Now all three are `Φ` applied to three elements of the SINGLE ring `Away 𝒜 t`. Since `Φ` is a ring hom, reduce to the algebra identity in `Away 𝒜 t`:  `awayMap(X k, t=(XiXj)Xk)(Xᵢ/Xⱼ) * awayMap(X i, t=(XjXk)Xi)(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = awayMap(X j, t=(XiXk)Xj)(Xᵢ/Xₖ)`  (all three `hx` proofs by `ring`/`mul_comm`/`mul_assoc`; note the target ring `Away 𝒜 t` is FIXED — supply `hx : t = f*g` proofs so every `awayMap` lands in `Away 𝒜 t`).
