data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ProjectiveSpace
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GlueDescent
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianQuot
10	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate
11	
12	/-!
13	# The Serre twisting sheaf `O(m)` on the integral model of projective space
14	
15	Mathlib v4.31 has no twisted structure sheaf on `Proj`.  This file constructs
16	`O(m)` on the integral model `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]` of projective space as a
17	`SheafOfModules`, by gluing the trivial line bundles on the basic opens
18	`D₊(Xᵢ)` along the transition units `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, through the project descent
19	engine `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue` (`Picard/GlueDescent.lean`) —
20	the same pattern as the Grassmannian universal quotient
21	(`Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean`), in rank one.
22	
23	Main definitions:
24	
25	* `ProjTwist.basicOpenCover n` — the open cover of `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ]` by the
26	  `D₊(Xᵢ)`;
27	* `ProjTwist.awayUnit n i j` — the unit `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` of the degree-zero localized
28	  ring `(ℤ[Xᵢ]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`, and `ProjTwist.overlapUnit n i j` its image in
29	  `Γ(V(i,j), O)` on the scheme-theoretic overlap of the cover;
30	* `ProjTwist.twistTransition n m i j` — the rank-one transition isomorphism
31	  multiplication by `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, conjugated by the structure-sheaf pullback
32	  comparison `Modules.pullbackUnitIso`;
33	* `ProjTwist.serreTwist n m` — `O(m)` on `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`, the glued
34	  sheaf of modules transported along the cover isomorphism `fromGlued`.
35	
36	The convention is that the transition *from* the `i`-th trivialisation *to*
37	the `j`-th multiplies by `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, corresponding to the local generator
38	`Xᵢ^m` of `O(m)` on `D₊(Xᵢ)`.
39	
40	Blueprint: `def:serre_twist`, `lem:serre_twist_transition_unit`,
41	`lem:serre_twist_cocycle` (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
42	-/
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial HomogeneousLocalization
45	open AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian (scalarEnd scalarEnd_one scalarEnd_comp)
46	
47	noncomputable section
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
50	
51	universe u
52	
53	/-! ## Rank-one pullback coherences
54	
55	The Serre-twist cocycle transports rank-one transition isomorphisms of the shape
56	`pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).symm` to a triple
57	overlap.  The next block is the rank-one mirror of the free-sheaf coherence stack
58	of `GrassmannianQuot.lean` / `GlueDescent.lean` (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`,
59	`matrixEnd_pullback`, the `pullbackCongr` endpoint absorptions): with
60	`SheafOfModules.unit` in place of `SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)`, `scalarEnd` in
61	place of `matrixEnd`, and `pullbackUnitIso` in place of `pullbackFreeIso`.  Each
62	is immediate from an already-proven unit-level atom. -/
63	
64	namespace Scheme.Modules
65	
66	open AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian (scalarEnd scalarEnd_pullback)
67	
68	/-- Rank-one `map_comp` coherence (analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_comp`): the
69	structure-sheaf pullback comparison at a composite `b ≫ a` factors through the
70	pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`.  Immediate from the unit coherence
71	`gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (`(pullbackUnitIso f).hom = pullbackObjUnitToUnit f`). -/
72	lemma pullbackUnitIso_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
73	    (pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
74	        (pullbackUnitIso (b ≫ a)).hom
75	      = (pullback b).map (pullbackUnitIso a).hom ≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).hom :=
76	  gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp a b
77	
78	/-- Iso-conjugated form of the scalar-endomorphism pullback atom `scalarEnd_pullback`:
79	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) = Q.hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) ≫ Q.inv`, with
80	`Q = pullbackUnitIso p`.  The rank-one analogue of `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
81	lemma scalarEnd_pullback_iso {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
82	    (pullback p).map (scalarEnd a)
83	      = (pullbackUnitIso p).hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) ≫ (pullbackUnitIso p).inv := by
84	  have key : (pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ (pullbackUnitIso p).hom
85	      = (pullbackUnitIso p).hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) :=
86	    scalarEnd_pullback p a
87	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← key, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
88	
89	/-- Closed zig-zag: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = 𝟙` for equal base
90	morphisms `φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr_hom`. -/
91	@[reassoc]
92	lemma pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr_hom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
93	    (pullbackUnitIso φ).inv ≫
94	        ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
95	        (pullbackUnitIso ψ).hom
96	      = 𝟙 _ := by
97	  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]
98	
99	/-- Left absorption: `pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = Q_φ` for equal base morphisms
100	`φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackCongr_hom_app_free`. -/
101	@[reassoc]
102	lemma pullbackCongr_hom_app_unit {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
103	    ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
104	        (pullbackUnitIso ψ).hom
105	      = (pullbackUnitIso φ).hom := by
106	  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]
107	
108	/-- Right absorption: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app = Q_ψ⁻¹` for equal base
109	morphisms `φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr`. -/
110	@[reassoc]
111	lemma pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
112	    (pullbackUnitIso φ).inv ≫
113	        ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom
114	      = (pullbackUnitIso ψ).inv := by
115	  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]
116	
117	end Scheme.Modules
118	
119	namespace ProjTwist
120	
121	variable (n : Type u)
122	
123	/-- The variable `Xᵢ` is homogeneous of degree `1`. -/
124	lemma X_mem_deg_one (i : n) :
125	    (X i : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) ∈ homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 1 :=
126	  isHomogeneous_X _ _
127	
128	/-- The product `XᵢXⱼ` is homogeneous of degree `2`. -/
129	lemma X_mul_X_mem_deg_two (i j : n) :
130	    (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) ∈
131	      homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 2 :=
132	  SetLike.mul_mem_graded (X_mem_deg_one n i) (X_mem_deg_one n j)
133	
134	/-- The basic opens `D₊(Xᵢ)` cover `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`: the variables
135	generate the coordinate ring over its degree-zero part. -/
136	lemma iSup_basicOpen_X_eq_top :
137	    ⨆ i, Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i) = ⊤ := by
138	  refine Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top' _ _ (fun i => ⟨1, X_mem_deg_one n i⟩) ?_
139	  rw [eq_top_iff]
140	  rintro a -
141	  have ha : a ∈ Algebra.adjoin (ULift.{u} ℤ)
142	      (Set.range (X : n → MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) := by
143	    rw [MvPolynomial.adjoin_range_X]; trivial
144	  induction ha using Algebra.adjoin_induction with
145	  | mem x hx => exact Algebra.subset_adjoin hx
146	  | algebraMap r =>
147	      have hr : (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) r
148	          ∈ homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0 := by
149	        rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
150	        exact isHomogeneous_C _ _
151	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem
152	        (Algebra.adjoin (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
153	          (Set.range (X : n → MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
154	        (⟨_, hr⟩ : homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
155	  | add x y _ _ hx hy => exact add_mem hx hy
156	  | mul x y _ _ hx hy => exact mul_mem hx hy
157	
158	/-- The open cover of the integral model by the basic opens `D₊(Xᵢ)`. -/
159	def basicOpenCover :
160	    (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))).OpenCover where
161	  I₀ := n
162	  X i := (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i)).toScheme
163	  f i := (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i)).ι
164	  mem₀ := by
165	    rw [Scheme.presieve₀_mem_precoverage_iff]
166	    refine ⟨fun x => ?_, inferInstance⟩
167	    have : x ∈ ⨆ i, Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i) := by
168	      rw [iSup_basicOpen_X_eq_top]; trivial
169	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp this
170	    exact ⟨i, ⟨x, hi⟩, rfl⟩
171	
172	/-- The glue datum of the basic-open cover of the integral model. -/
173	@[reducible] def glueData : Scheme.GlueData.{u} := (basicOpenCover n).gluedCover
174	
175	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xⱼ = Xᵢ²/(XᵢXⱼ)` as an element of the degree-zero
176	localized ring `(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`. -/
177	def awayFraction (i j : n) :
178	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) :=
179	  Away.mk _ (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i j) 1 (X i * X i)
180	    (by simpa using X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i i)
181	
182	/-- The fraction `Xⱼ/Xᵢ = Xⱼ²/(XᵢXⱼ)` in the *same* localized ring
183	`(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀` — the inverse of `awayFraction`. -/
184	def awayFractionInv (i j : n) :
185	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) :=
186	  Away.mk _ (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i j) 1 (X j * X j)
187	    (by simpa using X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n j j)
188	
189	lemma awayFraction_mul_inv (i j : n) :
190	    awayFraction n i j * awayFractionInv n i j = 1 := by
191	  apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
192	  rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_mul, HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
193	    awayFraction, awayFractionInv, Away.val_mk, Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_mul]
194	  have hnum : (X i * X i) * (X j * X j)
195	      = ((X i * X j) ^ 1 * (X i * X j) ^ 1 : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) := by
196	    ring
197	  rw [hnum]
198	  exact Localization.mk_self
199	    ((⟨(X i * X j) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
200	        Submonoid.powers (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) *
201	      (⟨(X i * X j) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
202	        Submonoid.powers (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
203	
204	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` as a unit of `(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`, with inverse
205	`Xⱼ/Xᵢ`. -/
206	def awayUnit (i j : n) :
207	    (Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j))ˣ where
208	  val := awayFraction n i j
209	  inv := awayFractionInv n i j
210	  val_inv := awayFraction_mul_inv n i j
211	  inv_val := (mul_comm _ _).trans (awayFraction_mul_inv n i j)
212	
213	/-! ## The transition units on the scheme-theoretic overlaps -/
214	
215	/-- The scheme-theoretic overlap of the glue datum factors through the basic
216	open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ) = D₊(Xᵢ) ⊓ D₊(Xⱼ)`. -/
217	lemma range_overlap_le (i j : n) :
218	    Set.range (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
219	        (basicOpenCover n).f i).base ⊆
220	      Set.range ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
221	        (X i * X j)).ι).base := by
222	  rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, Proj.basicOpen_mul]
223	  rintro _ ⟨v, rfl⟩
224	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
225	  · have hx : ((pullback.fst _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f i) v : Proj _) ∈
226	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
227	          (X i)).ι) := by
228	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
229	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
230	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
231	  · have hcond := congrArg (fun (φ : pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i)
232	        ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶ Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) =>
233	          φ.base v) pullback.condition
234	    simp only at hcond
235	    rw [hcond]
236	    have hx : ((pullback.snd _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f j) v : Proj _) ∈
237	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
238	          (X j)).ι) := by
239	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
240	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
241	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
242	
243	/-- The canonical morphism from the scheme-theoretic overlap of the `i`-th and
244	`j`-th charts to the basic open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ)`. -/
245	def overlapHom (i j : n) :
246	    pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶
247	      (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).toScheme :=
248	  IsOpenImmersion.lift
249	    ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).ι)
250	    (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
251	      (basicOpenCover n).f i)
252	    (range_overlap_le n i j)
253	
254	/-- The ring homomorphism carrying degree-zero fractions over `XᵢXⱼ` to
255	sections of the structure sheaf on the scheme-theoretic overlap. -/
256	def overlapRingHom (i j : n) :
257	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) →+*
258	      Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤) :=
259	  ((overlapHom n i j).appTop.hom.comp
260	    ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
261	      (X i * X j)).topIso.inv.hom)).comp
262	    (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).hom
263	
264	/-- The transition unit `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)` as a unit of the sections of the structure
265	sheaf on the scheme-theoretic overlap of the `i`-th and `j`-th charts. -/
266	def overlapUnit (i j : n) :
267	    Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤)ˣ :=
268	  Units.map (overlapRingHom n i j).toMonoidHom (awayUnit n i j)
269	
270	/-! ## Rank-one transitions -/
271	
272	/-- A unit of the global sections induces an automorphism of the structure
273	sheaf, multiplication by the unit (rank-one `matrixToFreeIso`). -/
274	def unitScalarIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} (v : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) :
275	    SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf where
276	  hom := scalarEnd v.val
277	  inv := scalarEnd v.inv
278	  hom_inv_id := by rw [scalarEnd_comp, v.val_inv, scalarEnd_one]
279	  inv_hom_id := by rw [scalarEnd_comp, v.inv_val, scalarEnd_one]
280	
281	@[simp]
282	lemma unitScalarIso_hom {Y : Scheme.{u}} (v : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) :
283	    (unitScalarIso v).hom = scalarEnd v.val := rfl
284	
285	@[simp]
286	lemma unitScalarIso_one {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
287	    unitScalarIso (1 : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) = Iso.refl _ :=
288	  Iso.ext (by rw [Iso.refl_hom]; exact (congrArg scalarEnd Units.val_one).trans scalarEnd_one)
289	
290	/-- Iso-level structure-sheaf-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms,
291	`pullbackUnitIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport
292	(the rank-one analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso`). -/
293	lemma pullbackUnitIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
294	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso φ ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ψ).symm
295	      = eqToIso (congrArg
296	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj
297	            (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)) h) := by
298	  subst h; simp
299	
300	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xᵢ` is `1`. -/
301	lemma awayFraction_self (i : n) : awayFraction n i i = 1 := by
302	  apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
303	  rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_one, awayFraction, Away.val_mk]
304	  have hden : (⟨(X i * X i) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
305	      Submonoid.powers (X i * X i : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))
306	      = ⟨X i * X i, 1, pow_one _⟩ := Subtype.ext (pow_one _)
307	  rw [hden]
308	  exact Localization.mk_self_mk _ _
309	
310	lemma awayUnit_self (i : n) : awayUnit n i i = 1 :=
311	  Units.ext (awayFraction_self n i)
312	
313	lemma overlapUnit_self (i : n) : overlapUnit n i i = 1 := by
314	  rw [overlapUnit, awayUnit_self, map_one]
315	
316	/-- The rank-one transition isomorphism of the Serre twist `O(m)`:
317	multiplication by `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, conjugated by the structure-sheaf pullback
318	comparisons on the scheme-theoretic overlap. -/
319	def twistTransition (m : ℕ) (i j : n) :
320	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((glueData n).f i j)).obj
321	        (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf) ≅
322	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).obj
323	        (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U j).ringCatSheaf) :=
324	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫
325	    unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i j) ^ m) ≪≫
326	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm
327	
328	/-- **(C1)** The diagonal transition of the Serre twist is the canonical
329	cast: `Xᵢ/Xᵢ = 1`, so the scalar automorphism is the identity and the two
330	pullback comparisons cancel. -/
331	lemma twistTransition_self (m : ℕ) (i : n) :
332	    twistTransition n m i i
333	      = eqToIso (congrArg
334	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
335	            (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf))
336	          (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
337	            rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])) := by
338	  have hmid : unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i i) ^ m)
339	      = Iso.refl (SheafOfModules.unit
340	          (pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f i)).ringCatSheaf) := by
341	    rw [overlapUnit_self, one_pow, unitScalarIso_one]
342	  calc twistTransition n m i i
343	      = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫
344	          unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i i) ^ m) ≪≫
345	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
346	            ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm := rfl
347	    _ = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫
348	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
349	            ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm := by
350	        refine (congrArg (fun e =>
351	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫ e ≪≫
352	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
353	              ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm) hmid).trans ?_
354	        exact congrArg (fun e => Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫ e)
355	          (Iso.refl_trans _)
356	    _ = eqToIso (congrArg
357	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
358	            (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf))
359	          (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
360	            rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])) :=
361	      pullbackUnitIso_trans_symm_eqToIso
362	        (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
363	          rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])
364	
365	/-- **Transport of a rank-one scalar automorphism through
366	`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the reusable abstract core of the Serre-twist
367	cocycle, the rank-one analogue of `pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso`
368	(`GrassmannianQuot.lean`).  A transition isomorphism of the twist shape
369	`pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).symm` (multiplication
370	by a unit `v` of `Γ(V, ⊤)`, conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along
371	`p : W ⟶ V` to the same shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, the scalar base-changed by
372	the comorphism `p.appTop`.  Combines the scalar-naturality atom
373	`scalarEnd_pullback_iso` with the pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackUnitIso_comp`. -/
374	lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
375	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) (v : Γ(V, ⊤)ˣ) :
376	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
377	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫
378	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b).symm)).hom
379	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ a)).hom ≫
380	        scalarEnd (p.appTop v.val) ≫
381	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)).inv := by
382	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
383	    Iso.symm_hom, unitScalarIso_hom]
384	  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the
385	  -- composite comparison `Q_{p≫a}` (pseudofunctoriality, `pullbackUnitIso_comp`).
386	  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
387	          (SheafOfModules.unit Yi.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
388	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso a).hom ≫
389	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p).hom
390	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ a)).hom := by
391	    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_comp a p]
392	    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
393	    rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]
394	  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into
395	  -- the inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`.
396	  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p).inv ≫
397	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b).inv ≫
398	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.unit Yj.ringCatSheaf)).hom
399	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)).inv := by
400	    have hiso : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.unit Yj.ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
401	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)
402	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b) ≪≫
403	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p := by
404	      apply Iso.ext
405	      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_comp b p
406	    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
407	    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv
408	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]
409	    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
410	    rw [Category.comp_id]
411	  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated scalar automorphism and apply the atom.
412	  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, Scheme.Modules.scalarEnd_pullback_iso]
413	  rw [← hfront, ← hback]
414	  rfl
415	
416	/-! ## The Serre twisting sheaf
417	
418	The descent engine `Scheme.Modules.glue` (`Picard/GlueDescent.lean`) is
419	universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}` (see the NOTE at
420	`GlueDescent.lean:934`), so the glued sheaf itself is only available for a
421	`Type 0` index — which covers the intended consumers `n = Fin (m + 1)`
422	(`ℙ^m`).  The cover, units and transitions above stay universe-polymorphic
423	for a future generalisation of the engine. -/
424	
425	section Universe0
426	
427	variable (n₀ : Type)
428	
429	/-- **Base cocycle of the transported overlap units** (`m = 1`): in
430	`Γ(V(i,j,k), O)`, the images of the three transition units `Xᵢ/Xⱼ`, `Xⱼ/Xₖ`,
431	`Xᵢ/Xₖ` under the base-change comorphisms to the common triple overlap satisfy
432	`p_IJ^♯(Xᵢ/Xⱼ) · p_JK^♯(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = p_IK^♯(Xᵢ/Xₖ)`.  This is the geometric heart of
433	the Serre-twist cocycle: it factors all three units through a common map
434	`V(i,j,k) ⟶ D₊(XᵢXⱼXₖ)` and reduces to the fraction identity
435	`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = Xᵢ/Xₖ` in `A⁰_{XᵢXⱼXₖ}` via `Proj.awayMap_awayToSection`. -/
436	lemma overlapUnit_cocycle_transport (i j k : n₀) :
437	    (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
438	        (overlapUnit n₀ i j).val *
439	      (Scheme.Hom.appTop ((glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫
440	          pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).f j i)))
441	        (overlapUnit n₀ j k).val
442	    = (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.snd ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
443	        (overlapUnit n₀ i k).val := by
444	  sorry
445	
446	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
447	/-- The `m`-th power form of the transported overlap cocycle, as consumed by
448	`twistTransition_cocycle`.  Immediate from `overlapUnit_cocycle_transport` since the
449	comorphisms are ring homomorphisms and `Γ(V(i,j,k), O)` is commutative
450	(`(Aᵐ)(Bᵐ) = (AB)ᵐ`).
451	
452	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`: the transported units carry
453	`pullback ((glueData n₀).f ..) ..` whose index `(glueData n₀).J` is `n₀` only up to
454	reducible transparency, so `rw`/`simp` motive-building must relax the instance wall. -/
455	lemma overlapUnit_cocycle_transport_pow (m : ℕ) (i j k : n₀) :
456	    (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
457	        ((overlapUnit n₀ i j ^ m).val) *
458	      (Scheme.Hom.appTop ((glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫
459	          pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).f j i)))
460	        ((overlapUnit n₀ j k ^ m).val)
461	    = (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.snd ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
462	        ((overlapUnit n₀ i k ^ m).val) := by
463	  simp only [Units.val_pow_eq_pow_val, map_pow, ← mul_pow, overlapUnit_cocycle_transport]
464	
465	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
466	/-- **(C2)** The triple-overlap cocycle condition for the Serre-twist
467	transitions, in the exact form consumed by `Scheme.Modules.glue`.  At the
468	level of transition units this is the identity
469	`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m (Xⱼ/Xₖ)^m = (Xᵢ/Xₖ)^m` in `Γ(V(i,j,k), O)`; the transport of the
470	conjugated scalar isomorphisms to the triple overlap runs through the
471	`pullbackUnitIso` coherences (the rank-one analogue of
472	`bundleTransition_cocycle_transport`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`).
473	
474	The engine `Scheme.Modules.glue` is universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`,
475	so the cocycle is proved here at `n₀ : Type` (the only regime that feeds the
476	glued sheaf); the rank-one scalar-transport core
477	`pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso` above stays generic. -/
478	lemma twistTransition_cocycle (m : ℕ) (i j k : n₀) :
479	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
480	        (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)) ((glueData n₀).f i j)
481	        ((glueData n₀).t i j ≫ (glueData n₀).f j i) (twistTransition n₀ m i j) ≪≫
482	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
483	        (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid (glueData n₀) i j k)).app
484	          (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n₀).U j).ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
485	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
486	        ((glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).f j i))
487	        ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).t j k ≫ (glueData n₀).f k j)
488	        (twistTransition n₀ m j k) ≪≫
489	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
490	        (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt (glueData n₀) i j k)).app
491	          (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n₀).U k).ringCatSheaf)
492	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
493	        (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src (glueData n₀) i j k)).app
494	          (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n₀).U i).ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
495	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
496	        (pullback.snd ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)) ((glueData n₀).f i k)
497	        ((glueData n₀).t i k ≫ (glueData n₀).f k i) (twistTransition n₀ m i k) := by
498	  apply Iso.ext
499	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, twistTransition]
500	  rw [pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso, pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso,
501	    pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso]
502	  simp only [Category.assoc]
503	  rw [Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr_hom_assoc
504	      (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid (glueData n₀) i j k),
505	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_hom_app_unit_assoc
506	      (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src (glueData n₀) i j k),
507	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr
508	      (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt (glueData n₀) i j k)]
509	  -- The endpoints (`Q_front`/`Q_back`) now match; fuse the two transported scalars via
510	  -- `scalarEnd_comp` and close with the base cocycle `overlapUnit_cocycle_transport`.
511	  rw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_comp,
512	    overlapUnit_cocycle_transport_pow n₀ m i j k]
513	
514	/-- The Serre twist `O(m)` on the glued total space of the basic-open cover. -/
515	def serreTwistGlued (m : ℕ) : (glueData n₀).glued.Modules :=
516	  Scheme.Modules.glue (glueData n₀)
517	    (fun i => SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n₀).U i).ringCatSheaf)
518	    (fun i j => twistTransition n₀ m i j)
519	    (fun i => twistTransition_self n₀ m i)
520	    (fun i j k => twistTransition_cocycle n₀ m i j k)
521	
522	/-- **The Serre twisting sheaf `O(m)`** on the integral model
523	`Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n₀]` of projective space: the glued sheaf transported along
524	the canonical isomorphism `fromGlued`. -/
525	def serreTwist (m : ℕ) :
526	    (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ))).Modules :=
527	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (inv (basicOpenCover n₀).fromGlued)).obj
528	    (serreTwistGlued n₀ m)
529	
530	end Universe0
531	
532	end ProjTwist
533	
534	end AlgebraicGeometry

1418	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop (Spec.map φ)

1507	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :

1509	        Scheme.Hom.appTop

1518	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom

1526	    rw [chartTransition']
1527	    simp only [Category.assoc]

1557	/-- **Base-change bridge to the localised cocycle, matrix form** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge`):
1558	over the triple overlap `V_IJK` the three geometrically base-changed Cramer inverses satisfy
1559	the multiplicative cocycle. The three projection bridges rewrite each base-changed matrix as
1560	the σ-image (`tripleOverlapSections`) of the corresponding L1 matrix, and the matrix-level
1561	cocycle `bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix` transports along the ring homomorphism σ.

1564	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1565	    (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1566	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))))
1567	          (chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
1568	            Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)))).mapMatrix
1569	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1570	            (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK))
1571	      * (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1572	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
1573	            (Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)
1574	              (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)))).mapMatrix
1575	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1576	              (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
1577	      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1578	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))
1579	            (Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)
1580	              (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)))).mapMatrix
1581	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1582	              (minorDet d r I K hI hK)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
1583	            (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK)) := by

1586	  have hT := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_transition d r I J K hI hJ hK)

1607	            ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)) := by

40	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
41	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
42	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
43	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
44	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
45	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
46	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
47	
48	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
49	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
50	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
51	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
52	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
53	
54	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
55	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
56	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
57	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
58	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
59	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
60	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
61	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
62	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
63	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
64	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
65	
66	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
67	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
68	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
69	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
70	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
71	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
72	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
73	
74	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
75	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
76	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
77	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
78	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
79	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
80	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
81	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
82	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
83	    Category.assoc]
84	
85	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
86	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
87	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
88	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
89	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
90	the module cocycle (C2). -/
91	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
92	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
93	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
94	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
95	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
96	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
97	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
98	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
99	
100	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
101	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
102	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
103	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
104	
105	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
106	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
107	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
108	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
109	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
110	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
111	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
112	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
113	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
114	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
115	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
116	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
117	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
118	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
119	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
120	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
121	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
122	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
123	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
124	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
125	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
126	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
127	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
128	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
129	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
130	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
131	    D.glued.Modules :=
132	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
133	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
134	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
135	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
136	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
137	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
138	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
139	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
140	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
141	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
142	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
143	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
144	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
145	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
146	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
147	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
148	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
149	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
150	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
151	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
152	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
153	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
154	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
155	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
156	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
157	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫

823	  refine RingHom.comp (e.symm.toRingHom.comp (Subring.inclusion ?_))
824	    (awayMapAux 𝒜 (f := f) ⟨_, hx⟩).rangeRestrict
825	  exact range_awayMapAux_subset 𝒜 hg hx
826	
827	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
828	set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
829	lemma val_awayMap_eq_aux (a) : (awayMap 𝒜 hg hx a).val = awayMapAux 𝒜 ⟨_, hx⟩ a := by
830	  let e := RingEquiv.ofLeftInverse (f := algebraMap (Away 𝒜 x) (Localization.Away x))
831	    (h := (val_injective _).hasLeftInverse.choose_spec)
832	  dsimp [awayMap]
833	  convert_to! (e (e.symm ⟨awayMapAux 𝒜 (f := f) ⟨_, hx⟩ a,
834	    range_awayMapAux_subset 𝒜 hg hx ⟨_, rfl⟩⟩)).1 = _
835	  rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
836	
837	lemma val_awayMap (a) : (awayMap 𝒜 hg hx a).val = Localization.awayLift (algebraMap A _) _
838	    (isUnit_of_dvd_unit (map_dvd _ ⟨_, hx⟩) (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit x)) a.val := by
839	  rw [val_awayMap_eq_aux]
840	  rfl
841	
842	lemma awayMap_fromZeroRingHom (a) :
843	    awayMap 𝒜 hg hx (fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _ a) = fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _ a := by
844	  ext
845	  simp only [fromZeroRingHom, RingHom.coe_mk, MonoidHom.coe_mk, OneHom.coe_mk,
846	    val_awayMap, val_mk]
847	  convert! IsLocalization.lift_eq _ _
848	
849	lemma val_awayMap_mk (n a i hi) : (awayMap 𝒜 hg hx (mk ⟨n, a, ⟨f ^ i, hi⟩, ⟨i, rfl⟩⟩)).val =
850	    Localization.mk (a * g ^ i) ⟨x ^ i, (Submonoid.mem_powers_iff _ _).mpr ⟨i, rfl⟩⟩ := by
851	  rw [val_awayMap_eq_aux, awayMapAux_mk 𝒜 (hx := hx)]
852	
853	/-- Given `x = f * g` with `g` homogeneous of positive degree,
854	this is the map `A_{(f)} → A_{(x)}` taking `a/f^i` to `ag^i/(fg)^i`. -/
855	def awayMapₐ : Away 𝒜 f →ₐ[𝒜 0] Away 𝒜 x where
856	  __ := awayMap 𝒜 hg hx
857	  commutes' _ := awayMap_fromZeroRingHom ..
858	
859	@[simp] lemma awayMapₐ_apply (a) : awayMapₐ 𝒜 hg hx a = awayMap 𝒜 hg hx a := rfl
860	
861	open SetLike in
862	@[simp]
863	lemma awayMap_mk {d : ι} (n : ℕ) (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 d) (a : A) (ha : a ∈ 𝒜 (n • d)) :
864	    awayMap 𝒜 hg hx (Away.mk 𝒜 hf n a ha) = Away.mk 𝒜 (hx ▸ mul_mem_graded hf hg) n
865	      (a * g ^ n) (by rw [smul_add]; exact mul_mem_graded ha (pow_mem_graded n hg)) := by
866	  ext
867	  exact val_awayMap_mk ..
868	
869	end mapAway
